title: Returning an object of the wrong type from a static function of a parameterized class
body:
Hello!

A error occurs when trying to return an object of a parameterized class from a static function declared in the same class: the type of the returned object does not match the expected type.

The expected behavior is no errors.

Related (but my example does not use external definitions) issue: https://github.com/verilator/verilator/issues/4924 (see `Test 1` output)

**Example:**

```systemverilog
module top;

  class cls#(bit T = 1);
    static function cls#(T) f();
      cls#(T) c = new();
      return c;
    endfunction
  endclass

  initial begin
    cls#(0) c = cls#(0)::f();
  end

endmodule
```

**Output:**

```text
%Error: top.sv:11:26: Assign RHS expects a CLASSREFDTYPE 'cls__T0', got CLASSREFDTYPE 'cls__Tz1'
                    : ... note: In instance 'top'
   11 |     cls#(0) c = cls#(0)::f();
      |                          ^
%Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s)
```

**Command:**

```bash
verilator --binary top.sv
```

---

**Workaround:**

There is a workaround for this case: use `type(this)` as return types in function `f` (IEEE 1800-2023 "6.23 Type operator").

```systemverilog
module top;

  class cls#(bit T = 1);
    static function type(this) f();
      type(this) c = new();
      return c;
    endfunction
  endclass

  initial begin
    cls#(0) c = cls#(0)::f();
  end

endmodule
```

---

Verilator version (git master): `Verilator 5.029 devel rev v5.028-114-g0ee214a15`

OS (verilator devcontainer): Ubuntu 22.04.4 LTS
